summary: NATO's first Quantum Technologies Strategy, approved in 2023 and summarised in a 2024 official text, sets the end-state of transitioning Alliance cryptographic systems to quantum-safe cryptography and commits Allies and NATO bodies to develop and implement post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution. NATO has described this work publicly since at least 2022. The NCI Agency and the NATO Cyber Security Centre have tested a hybrid post-quantum virtual private network combining post-quantum and traditional algorithms and proposed it to the IETF for open standardisation, while Science for Peace and Security projects, Allied Command Transformation and the DIANA accelerator carry related work. The included documents are a strategy summary, news articles, an SPS report, press releases and a challenge notice rather than binding law, and they name no FIPS family, named algorithm or completion date, so the Alliance posture reads as a stated direction of travel rather than a mandated and dated migration.
migrationTimeline:
2022 | NATO describes Alliance work on post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution to secure communications against the quantum threat
2024 | NATO adopts its first Quantum Technologies Strategy (approved 2023), setting a quantum-safe end-state for Alliance cryptographic systems and committing Allies and NATO bodies to post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution
govActors:
NATO | Adopts the Quantum Technologies Strategy committing Allies and NATO bodies to post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution
NCI Agency / NATO Cyber Security Centre | Tested a hybrid post-quantum VPN combining post-quantum and traditional algorithms and proposed it to the IETF for open standardisation
NCI Agency | Reports NATO quantum-safe work on post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution to enhance network resilience
NATO SPS | Science for Peace and Security Programme, funds post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution projects, including a project with Malta over submarine optical fibre
NATO ACT | Allied Command Transformation, work on quantum-resistant cryptography for defence, including a post-quantum cryptography experiment for friend-or-foe identification of tactical drones
NATO DIANA | Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic, challenge featuring communications with integrated post-quantum cryptography
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (NATO) + migrationTimeline 2024: Summary of NATO's Quantum Technologies Strategy and the first-quantum-strategy news article, setting the quantum-safe end-state (included results rows)
